calendar.matters:
Petition For Instructing Trustee
Off Calendar Mar-12-2026 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Gary Rothstein for trustee U.S. Bank, N.A. (via Zoom).
Wendy Krog for objector and beneficiary Mark Ridgeway (via Zoom).
Matthew Ridgeway, beneficiary (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on Petition for Order Instructing Trustee.

The Court heard argument regarding the trustee's request for instructions concerning a promissory note secured by real property that may be subject to environmental contamination. Ms. Krog argued in opposition to the petition and opposed distributing the note in kind to the beneficiaries. Mr. Rothstein responded that the trustee had only recently learned of potential environmental contamination issues affecting the property and that foreclosure could result in the trust taking title to contaminated property and bearing remediation costs.

After hearing argument, the Court stated that it had reviewed the filings and found that the objector had not met the burden of demonstrating that the trustee's proposed action should not be taken. The Court further noted that issues concerning any potential surcharge or breach of fiduciary duty were not before the Court on this petition.

The Court took the matter under submission with the intent to grant the petition.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition For Instructing Trustee
Off Calendar Feb-03-2026 Continued To Mar-10-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Gary Rothstein appeared via zoom for U.S. Bank, N.A. (petitioner).
Wendy Krog appeared via zoom for and with Mark Ridgeway (objector).
Catherine Brewer appeared via zoom (pro per) (objector).

Ms. Krog requested a brief continuance to allow parties review the objections filed, that other beneficiaries did not wan to be apart of the objections she filed on behalf of her client.

Mr. Rothstein stated that he prefers to meet and confer with counsel, and that they wouldn't have sufficient time for mediation.

The matter is continued to March 10, 2026 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Written objections are due by March 3, 2026.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Obitinalla. Not reported.
